revision.text:
[[ja:白山中居神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = Hakusan Chukyō Shrine |image = File:Hakusanchukyo Shinto shrine 2011-04-17.jpg |Location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}{{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|qid=Q857243|lt=Gujo City|1=Gujō, Gifu}} Hakuchō-chō Ishitoshiro 3-48 |Coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|59|31.5|N|136|46|7.1|E|display=title,inline}} |Deities = {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|1=Kikurihime}}, {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|lt=Izanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}, {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|lt=Izanami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} |Status = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}・{{ill|Kinkei Shrine|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkei Shrine|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}} |Founded = Traditionally, the 12th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (82 AD) |Main Hall = Fukuya-style (覆屋造) |Also known as = |Sacred Sites = |Annual Festivals = May 15; October 20 |Rituals = Iwakyo Ritual; Gedo Nage Festival; Five-tier Kagura |Map = {{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=Hakusan
Chukyō Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|59|31.5|N|136|46|7.1|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|35|59|31.5|N|136|46|7.1|E}}}} |prefecture= {{ill|Gujō|qid=Q857243|1=Gujō, Gifu}} |coordinates= {{Coord|35.992092|N|136.768653|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Hakusan Chukyō Shrine''' ({{ill|白山中居神社|ja|白山中居神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/白山中居神社|1=Hakusan Nakai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579553}}) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|qid=Q857243|lt=Gujo City|1=Gujō, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, in the area of Hakuchō, Ishitoshiro. == Overview == {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} is one of the shrines of the Hakusan cult and is deeply associated with {{ill|Hakusan Faith|ja|白山信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Faith|qq=}}. The route from {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}} to Hakusan passes from {{ill|Hakusan Chūgū Nagataki-dera|ja|白山中宮長滝寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Chūgū Nagataki-dera|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504608|1=Hakusan Chūgū Nagataki-dera Temple}} (the present-day {{ill|Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja|長滱白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505741|1=Nagataki Hakusan Shrine}} and {{ill|Nagataki-dera|ja|長瀧寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagataki-dera|1=Chōryū-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11653375}}) through the {{ill|Hakusan Chukyō Shrine|ja|白山中居神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Chukyō Shrine|1=Hakusan Nakai Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11579553}} of {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}, en route to {{ill|Mount Haku|en|Mount Haku|qid=Q2639098|lt=Hakusan|1=Mount Haku}} – the center of Hakusan {{ill|Faith|en|Faith|qid=Q5410500|lt=Faith|1=Faith}}, along with {{ill|Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja|長滱白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505741|1=Nagataki Hakusan Shrine}} and {{ill|Suhara Shrine|ja|洲原神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suhara Shrine|1=Suhara Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11556504}} in the Mino Zenjōdō. The shrine is situated on the left bank of the {{ill|Itoshiro River|en|Itoshiro River|qid=Q6094870|lt=Itoshiro River|1=Itoshiro River}}, at the confluence with its tributary, the Miyagawa. A bridge spans the approach to the shrine.{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.itoshiro.jp/area_gd.html |title=石徹白の釣り場マップ |publisher=石徹白漁業協同組合 |accessdate=2011-04-19}} == Deities == '''Main Enshrined Deities''' * {{ill|Kikurihime|en|Kikurihime|qid=Q11619891|lt=Kikurihime-no-Kami|1=Kikurihime}} (also known as {{ill|Hakusan Hime-no-Kami|ja|白山比咩神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Hime-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 菊理媛神|1=Kikurihime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11619891}}・{{ill|Hakusan Gongen|ja|白山権現|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Gongen|qq=}}) * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|lt=Izanagi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊弉冉尊|lt=Izanami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q682306}} '''Deities of the Shrine Precincts''' * {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Oohisachi-no-Mikoto|1=Amaterasu}} (in the Niiname-sha) * {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|en|Iwanaga-hime|qid=Q8191705|lt=Iwanagahime|1=Iwanaga-hime}} (in the Imperial Ancestral Shrine) * {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|en|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qid=Q272993|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}} (at Suga Shrine) * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} (at Jizō Shrine) * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|en|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} (at the Dōso Shrine) == History == In {{ill|Itoshiro Village|ja|石徹白村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshiro Village|1=Itoshiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586524}}, people have inhabited the area since 9000 years ago during the {{ill|Japanese Paleolithic|ja|旧石器時代 (日本)|lt=9000 years ago|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Paleolithic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本列島の旧石器時代|1=Japanese Paleolithic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q844172}}, and since the {{ill|Jōmon period|en|Jōmon period|qid=Q52824|lt=Jomon Period|1=Jōmon period}}, primitive {{ill|Ko-Shintō|en|Ko-Shintō|qid=Q3096508|lt=Ancient Shinto|1=Ko-Shintō}} {{ill|rituals|ja|祭祀 (神道)|lt=Rituals|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rituals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Shinto ritual|qid=Q109937652}} were observed at the sacred stone seat (rendered as {{ill|Iwakura rock|en|Iwakura rock|qid=Q11587880|lt=Iwakyo|1=Iwakura rock}}) where the descent of {{ill|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|en|Kuni-no-Tokotachi|qid=Q478504|lt=Kuninotokotachi|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} was revered. According to tradition, the shrine was established in the 12th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (82 AD), and in the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} (465 AD) an imperial envoy was sent to dedicate a sword for national protection. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, {{ill|Yoshibikihiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshibikihiko|1=Kibi no Takehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18226257}} (also rendered as Takehiko) received an oracle in the mountains: > "An ill-disposed bandit—unworthy to be presented to my imperial offspring—rises in all directions. I shall now descend to Funaoka to protect my imperial descendants. Stay with me long; the shrine where I abide lies in the midst of Funaoka, between the mountains where the morning sun alone shines and the mountains where the evening sun glows, between waterfalls." On the 12th day of the New Year in the 12th year (82 AD) of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, in accordance with the oracle, the land of Funaoka Chukyō was leveled and a palace was built; on June 15 of the same year, the deity was enshrined in the newly constructed hall.{{Cite web|和書 |title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=300}} The shrine building was said to measure 7 shaku (approximately 2.10 m) in length and 2 jō 1 shaku (approximately 6.30 m) in height.{{Cite web |title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/8 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |work=白山中居神社略史 |year=1912 |editor=上杉富麿 |author=上杉富麿 |page=8 |language=日本語}} In the 40th year of Emperor Keikō (110 AD), when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} undertook the expedition against the eastern barbarians alongside {{ill|Yoshibikihiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshibikihiko|1=Kibi no Takehiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18226257}}, he stopped by {{ill|Itoshiro Village|ja|石徹白村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshiro Village|1=Itoshiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586524}} to revere the divine spirit of the shrine.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |work=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |page=300 |date=1912}} In the 4th year of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|qid=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}} (316 AD), when epidemics spread throughout the land, two deities—the one enshrined at {{ill|Izumo-taisha|en|Izumo-taisha|qid=Q696362|lt=Kine Tsuki|1=Izumo-taisha}} (for Susanoo) and {{ill|Ōkuninushi|en|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Okuninushi|1=Ōkuninushi}} at Kine Tsuki—were transferred to Itoshiro and enshrined (currently at Suga Shrine and Jizō Shrine).{{Cite web|和書 |title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |page=300 |work=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |editor=福井県大野郡教育会 |author=福井県大野郡教育会 |date=1912}} In the 9th year of {{ill|Emperor Yūryaku|en|Emperor Yūryaku|qid=Q357235|lt=Emperor Yuryaku|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} (465 AD), during the festival of {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}}, a minister ({{ill|Minister|ja|大臣 (古代日本)|lt=Minister|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minister|1=Ōomi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4328809}}) {{ill|Heguri no Matori|en|Heguri no Matori|qid=Q11483500|lt=Hirahoromadotori|1=Heguri no Matori}} was sent with offerings, and a sword was dedicated for national protection. Together with {{ill|Otomi Hikomyo|ja|大途見彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomi Hikomyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Otomi Narihiko|ja|大途見根彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomi Narihiko|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he proceeded to the shrine where, because he was then called {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukunabikona|1=Sukune}}, the place was named Inohara.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=300 |language=ja}} In the 1st year of {{ill|Emperor Kenzō|en|Emperor Kenzō|qid=Q360966|lt=Emperor Kenzō|1=Emperor Kenzō}} (485 AD), the deity of the five grains was enshrined.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=300}} During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|en|Emperor Tenmu|qid=Q335181|lt=Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} (673–686 AD), sacred items including a divine sword, bow, arrows, spears, shield, and sacred horse were bestowed upon the shrine; the sword dedicated by Emperor Tenmu was still extant as of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 45.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=300}} In the 1st year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yoro|1=Yōrō}} (717 AD), {{ill|Taichō|en|Taichō|qid=Q2388439|lt=Taichō Daisō|1=Taichō}} expanded the shrine’s grounds and restored the shrine building.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/301 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=301}} In the 7th year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} era (735 AD), {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} sent {{ill|Kibi no Makibi|en|Kibi no Makibi|qid=Q1063276|lt=Kibi no Makibi|1=Kibi no Makibi}} as his envoy to dedicate a divine sword. At that time, the shrine officials were commanded never to cut down the trees on the shrine grounds. It was also then that the inscription changed from “石度白” to “石徹白” at the suggestion of Makibi.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/301 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=301}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Ninju|en|Ninju|qid=Q1373077|lt=Jinju|1=Ninju}} (853 AD), {{ill|Emperor Montoku|en|Emperor Montoku|qid=Q357225|lt=Emperor Buntei|1=Emperor Montoku}} conferred the Junior Third Rank.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |work=白山中居神社略史 |year=1913 |author=上杉富麿 |editor=上杉富麿 |page=9}} In the 1st year of the {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} era (859 AD) on January 27, {{ill|Emperor Seiwa|en|Emperor Seiwa|qid=Q349243|lt=Emperor Seiwa|1=Emperor Seiwa}} conferred the Junior Third Rank.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/300 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=300}} During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Uda|en|Emperor Uda|qid=Q349267|lt=Emperor Uda|1=Emperor Uda}} (887–897 AD), the head priest of the shrine, Yasushi Inohara, was promoted first to the Senior Fifth Rank and later to the Junior Fourth Rank.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/301 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育委員会 |date=1912 |page=301}} In the 2nd year of the {{ill|Tentoku|en|Tentoku|qid=Q1327662|lt=Tendoku|1=Tentoku}} era (958 AD), the Minister of Right, {{ill|Fujiwara no Morosuke|en|Fujiwara no Morosuke|qid=Q325207|lt=Fujiwara no Morosuke|1=Fujiwara no Morosuke}}, offered prayers at the shrine.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |work=白山中居神社略史 |year=1913 |author=上杉富麿 |editor=上杉富麿 |page=9}} From the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian Period|1=Heian period}} through the early {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo Period|1=Edo period}}, many military commanders—including {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Nōshin|ja|藤原能信|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Nōshin|1=Fujiwara no Yoshinobu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623489}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidehira|en|Fujiwara no Hidehira|qid=Q3090625|lt=Fujiwara no Hidetaka|1=Fujiwara no Hidehira}}, {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|en|Imagawa Yoshimoto|qid=Q1054305|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}}, {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}, {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, and {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}}—sought the favor of Hakusan Faith. During the Edo Period, {{ill|Echizen Province|en|Echizen Province|qid=Q1047144|lt=Echizen Province|1=Echizen Province}}’s {{ill|Ōno District, Fukui|en|Ōno District, Fukui|qid=Q8081656|lt=Ono District, Fukui|1=Ōno District, Fukui}} and {{ill|Itoshiro Village|ja|石徹白村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshiro Village|1=Itoshiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586524}} aided pilgrims on the journey to {{ill|Mount Haku|en|Mount Haku|qid=Q2639098|lt=Hakusan|1=Mount Haku}}. The villagers became affiliates of the shrine, enjoying tax exemption and the privilege of bearing swords. In the 2nd year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Juei|1=Keichō}} (1183 AD), {{ill|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|en|Minamoto no Yoshinaka|qid=Q1139202|lt=Minamoto no Yoshinaka|1=Minamoto no Yoshinaka}} offered prayers for the defeat of the Heike.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23 |work=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |page=303 |author=福井県大野郡教育会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育会}} In the 7th year of {{ill|Kōan (Kamakura period)|en|Kōan (Kamakura period)|qid=Q389180|lt=Kōan|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} (1284 AD), {{ill|Nyoin|ja|女院|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nyoin|1=nyoin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10075498}} (also known as the Female Imperial Retainer) {{ill|Fujiwara no Tadako|ja|五辻忠子|lt=Fujiwara Tadako|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tadako|1=Fujiwara no Chūshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372683}} offered prayers and dedicated a treasure mirror.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |work=白山中居神社略史 |year=1913 |author=上杉富麿 |editor=上杉富麿 |page=9}} In the 2nd year of {{ill|Genryaku|en|Genryaku|qid=Q1194250|lt=Genryaku|1=Genryaku}} (1185 AD), retainers Sakurai Heishiryo Masayoshi of {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidehira|en|Fujiwara no Hidehira|qid=Q3090625|lt=Fujiwara no Hidetaka|1=Fujiwara no Hidehira}} and Uesugi Takeemon Sōyō from the {{ill|Northern Fujiwara|en|Northern Fujiwara|qid=Q1050942|lt=Ōshū Fujiwara clan|1=Northern Fujiwara}} visited {{ill|Itoshiro Village|ja|石徹白村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itoshiro Village|1=Itoshiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586524}}, where upper and lower shrines were established to enshrine a bronze seated statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}}. It is said that the Uesugi clan of Itoshiro are the descendants of those who accompanied them; after a brief return to {{ill|Historic Monuments and Sites of Hiraizumi|en|Historic Monuments and Sites of Hiraizumi|qid=Q2896261|lt=Hiraizumi|1=Historic Monuments and Sites of Hiraizumi}}, they relocated with their families to Itoshiro to guard the bronze statue.{{Cite web|title=中日新聞「藤原秀衡ゆかりの岐阜県郡上市「石徹白大師堂」の虚空蔵菩薩像」|url=https://plus.chunichi.co.jp/blog/tanaka/article/288/11192/ |website=plus.chunichi.co.jp |access-date=2024-05-24 |author=田中ひろみ |editor=中日新聞 |date=2023-08-28}} In the 9th year of the {{ill|Tenbun|en|Tenbun|qid=Q1192853|lt=Tenmon|1=Tenbun}} era (1540 AD), {{ill|Imagawa Yoshimoto|en|Imagawa Yoshimoto|qid=Q1054305|lt=Imagawa Yoshimoto|1=Imagawa Yoshimoto}} offered prayers.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06 |publisher=国立国会図書館 |work=白山中居神社略史 |year=1913 |author=上杉富麿 |editor=上杉富麿 |page=9}} At that time, a lodging facility for mountain ascetics was constructed.{{Cite web|和書|title=越前及若狭地方の史蹟 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1208877/1/190 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |work=越前及若狭地方の史蹟 |author=上田三平 |page=190 |language=ja}} In the 2nd year of {{ill|Genki (era)|en|Genki (era)|qid=Q1145064|lt=Genki|1=Genki (era)}} (1571 AD), {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}} donated three bronze {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|qid=Q11674315|lt=Wani-kuchi|1=Waniguchi}} (existing today).{{Cite web|和書|title=越前及若狭地方の史蹟 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1208877/1/191 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |author=上田三平 |date=1933 |page=191 |language=ja |work=越前及若狭地方の史蹟}} In the 1st year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1973 AD), on June 7, {{ill|Asakura Kageaki|en|Asakura Kageaki|qid=Q4803439|lt=Asakura Keikagami|1=Asakura Kageaki}} visited and dedicated 20 kan of gold.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23 |work=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |page=303 |author=福井県大野郡教育会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育会}} In the 4th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1976 AD), on a favorable day in June, {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}} donated a {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|qid=Q11674315|lt=Wani-kuchi|1=Waniguchi}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23}} In the 18th year of {{ill|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|en|Tenshō (Momoyama period)|qid=Q1145058|lt=Tenshō Era|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} (1590 AD), the Toyotomi clan, led by {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|qid=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}, began their pilgrimage to Hakusan via the Chukyō Shrine.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23}} In the 6th year of {{ill|Keichō|en|Keichō|qid=Q1328185|lt=Keichō|1=Keichō}} (1601 AD), on November 23, Itō Hidemori, a vassal of the Toyotomi family and governor of {{ill|Ishikawa District, Ishikawa|en|Ishikawa District, Ishikawa|qid=Q1155548|lt=Ishikawa District|1=Ishikawa District, Ishikawa}}, donated land (30 koku) in the Enshu region around {{ill|Hamamatsu|ja|浜松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hamamatsu|1=Hamamatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11557732}}, with the domain lord {{ill|Matsudaira Tadayori|en|Matsudaira Tadayori|qid=Q6788073|lt=Matsudaira Tadayori|1=Matsudaira Tadayori}} serving as a witness.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23}} From the establishment of the {{ill|Tokugawa shogunate|en|Tokugawa shogunate|qid=Q205662|lt=Edo Shogunate|1=Tokugawa shogunate}} by {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} up to {{ill|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|en|Tokugawa Yoshinobu|qid=Q287657|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinobu|1=Tokugawa Yoshinobu}}, successive shōgun regularly visited the shrine to offer prayers and perform rituals administered by priests.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06}} The schedule was as follows: from July 3 to 6, on the 3rd the pilgrims visited the shrine at Itoshiro to pray for enduring military success; on the 4th they ascended Hakusan; on the 5th a ritual was held at Tenrei; and on the 6th they returned to Itoshiro.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/303 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-24 |author=福井県大野郡教育会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育会 |page=303}} In the 18th year of {{ill|Kyōhō|en|Kyōhō|qid=Q1145048|lt=Kyōhō|1=Kyōhō}} (1733 AD), the shrine was conferred the Senior First Rank.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06}} Between the 4th and 8th years of {{ill|Hōreki|en|Hōreki|qid=Q1194397|lt=Hōreki|1=Hōreki}} (1754–1758 AD), the Itoshiro Disturbance occurred. The head priest of Hakusan Chukyō Shrine, Uemura Toyomae, colluded with Neō Jinzaemon, the temple and shrine magistrate of the Gujo domain, and with Sugimoto Sakon, the administrator of the shrine territory, expelling about 500 villagers from Itoshiro Village. In conjunction with the Gujo Uprising, judgments were rendered by the shogunate’s council; Senior Councilor Honda Masachin and Elder Honda Tadao were dismissed and their lands confiscated, and domain lord Kanamori Yorikin’s estates were also confiscated, resulting in large-scale penalties including the disinheritance of families.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/302 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-23}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Meiwa|en|Meiwa|qid=Q1146161|lt=Meiwa|1=Meiwa}} (1766 AD), {{ill|Seikōmonin|ja|青綺門院|lt=Seikōmonin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seikōmonin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二条舎子|1=Nijō Ieko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7141648}} and {{ill|Nijō Shako|ja|二条舎子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nijō Shako|1=Nijō Ieko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q7141648}} dedicated a family register bearing the Imperial Crest.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06}} In the 3rd year of {{ill|Ansei|en|Ansei|qid=Q1327259|lt=Ansei|1=Ansei}} (1856 AD), the present Main Hall was rebuilt. In the 1st year of {{ill|Man'en|en|Man'en|qid=Q1193862|lt=Man'en|1=Man'en}} (1860 AD), the shrine was granted the title of Great Shrine.{{Cite web|和書|title=白山中居神社略史 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/911332/1/9 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-06-06}} In the 1st year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1868 AD), during the initial period of the {{ill|Shinbutsu bunri|en|Shinbutsu bunri|qid=Q1970535|lt=Shinbutsu Bunri|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, a Daishidō was constructed to house Buddhist images and implements from Hakusan Chukyō Shrine.{{Cite web|和書|title=石徹白太師堂 {{!}} 地域資源デジタルアーカイブによる知の拠点形成のための基盤整備事業 |url=https://digitalarchiveproject.jp/information/%e5%a4%aa%e5%ad%90%e5%a0%82/ |date=2018-07-24 |access-date=2023-06-26 |language=ja}} In the 40th year of {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} (1907 AD) on May 3, seven shrines and chapels in Itoshiro Village were amalgamated. Each shrine is traditionally said to have been founded in either the 6th or 9th month of the first year of {{ill|Yōrō|en|Yōrō|qid=Q1373061|lt=Yoro|1=Yōrō}}.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/304 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-24 |work=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |pages=303-304 |author=福井県大野郡教育会 |editor=福井県大野郡教育会}} * For the shrines without separate names – Kamihato Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Amenouzume|ja|天鈿女命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amenouzume|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノウズメ|1=Ame-no-Uzume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462803}}; located in Minasu. * For the shrines without separate names – Kotani-do Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Hikohahide-no-Kami|ja|彦火々見出神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikohahide-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; located in Kamiyamaguchi. * For the shrines without separate names – Mizu-tomi Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Ounomikusa-fukugō Shinkami|ja|鸕鶿草葺不合神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ounomikusa-fukugō Shinkami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; located on Ninofuse, Hakusan. * For the shrines without separate names – Shinmei Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Amaterasu|ja|天照皇大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}}; located in Shinmei. * For the shrines without separate names – Bijo-shita Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Iwanagahime|ja|磐長媛命|lt=Iwanagahime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanagahime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワナガヒメ|1=Iwanaga-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8191705}}; located in Miyanoo. * For the shrines without separate names – Ima Shimizu Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Kunu-no-Mikoto|ja|瓊瓊杵神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunu-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}; located in Kawaurē. * For the shrines without separate names – Ichinose Shrine, enshrining {{ill|Kunado-no-Kami|ja|久那斗の神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunado-no-Kami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐の神|1=Kunado-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2963653}}; located in Ichinose. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 43 (1910 AD), on October 1, two shrines in {{ill|Shimoanabamura|ja|下穴馬村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoanabamura|1=Shimoanama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17176531}}—which in May of the same year had been designated as eligible to receive offerings of Shinsen Heihaku-ryō—were merged.{{Cite web|和書|title=福井県大野郡誌 下編 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1088300/1/304 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2024-05-24}} * Village Shrine: {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}}, enshrining {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}; located in Chōjitsusakamura-no-Uchi. * Village Shrine: {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}}, enshrining {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊弉諾尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}; located in Kakuranomasakasamura-no-ue. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 44 (1911 AD) on July 10, {{ill|Kunio Yanagita|en|Kunio Yanagita|qid=Q971687|lt=Yanagita Kunio|1=Kunio Yanagita}} visited and stayed at the home of Uemura Gobei in Nakai-dokoro.{{Cite web|和書|title=柳田国男先生著作集 第６冊 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1450612/1/105 |website=dl.ndl.go.jp |access-date=2023-06-23 |work=柳田国男先生著作集 第６冊 |date=1948 |page=105 |language=ja |author=柳田国男 |editor=実業之日本社}} In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 10 (1921 AD), the shrine became a Prefectural Shrine of {{ill|Fukui Prefecture|en|Fukui Prefecture|qid=Q133879|lt=Fukui Prefecture|1=Fukui Prefecture}}. In {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 33 (1958 AD), Itoshiro Village was transferred from Fukui Prefecture to become part of {{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|qid=Q857243|lt=Gujo City|1=Gujō, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}}. In {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 37 (1962 AD), the carvings on the Main Hall were designated as an Important Cultural Property by {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|qid=Q131277|lt=Gifu Prefecture|1=Gifu Prefecture}},{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/5580.html |title=中居神社本殿の彫刻 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-24}} and the shrine attained the status of {{ill|Kinkei Shrine|ja|金幣社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinkei Shrine|1=Kinpei-sha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929592}}. == Rituals == * New Year Three-Day Festival * Third Sunday of May – Spring Festival ** Since 1120 (the 1st year of the {{ill|Hōan|en|Hōan|qid=Q1194464|lt=Hōan|1=Hōan}} era), in accordance with the tradition of gagaku, two shrine maidens perform five types of dances, including the Five-tier Kagura – a designated Important {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24895900}} of {{ill|Gujō, Gifu|en|Gujō, Gifu|qid=Q857243|lt=Gujo City|1=Gujō, Gifu}}.{{Cite web |url=https://tabitabigujo.com/spots/shirotori/%E7%99%BD%E5%B1%B1%E4%B8%AD%E5%B1%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E6%98%A5%E5%AD%A3%E4%BE%8B%E7%A5%AD/ |title=白山中居神社春季例祭 |access-date=2024-10-26 |publisher=一般社団法人 郡上市観光連盟 |website=TABITABI郡上}} * Third Sunday of July – Founding Festival ** Before the establishment of the ancient Chukyō Shrine, in front of the sacred rock cave where the great deity dwelled, a maiden performed the “Urayasu Dance” and the “Maiden Dance”.{{Cite web |url=https://tabitabigujo.com/spots/shirotori/%E7%99%BD%E5%B1%B1%E4%B8%AD%E5%B1%85%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE%E5%89%B5%E6%A5%AD%E7%A5%AD/ |title=白山中居神社創業祭 |access-date=2024-10-26 |publisher=一般社団法人 郡上市観光連盟 |website=TABITABI郡上}} == Gallery == Hakusanchukyo-jinja trii.JPG|{{ill|Torii|en|Torii|qid=Q234731|lt=Torii|1=Torii}} Hakusanchukyo-jinja s3.JPG|{{ill|Main Palace|ja|大宮殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hakusanchukyo-jinja s5.JPG|{{ill|Suga Shrine|ja|須賀社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suga Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Hakusanchukyo-jinja honden s3.JPG|{{ill|Main Hall|ja|御本殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == === Nationally Designated Important Cultural Properties === * Bronze Seated Statue of {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu|1=Ākāśagarbha}} (donated by {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidehira|en|Fujiwara no Hidehira|qid=Q3090625|lt=Fujiwara no Hidetaka|1=Fujiwara no Hidehira}}) === Gifu Prefecture Designated Important Cultural Properties === * Carvings on the Main Hall of the shrine * {{ill|Noh Mask|ja|能面|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noh Mask|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Noh mask|qid=Q11612081}}{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12565.html |title=県指定有用文化財　白山中居神社　能面 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-05-11}} * Bronze {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|qid=Q11674315|lt=Wani-kuchi|1=Waniguchi}} (donated by {{ill|Oda Nobunaga|en|Oda Nobunaga|qid=Q171411|lt=Oda Nobunaga|1=Oda Nobunaga}}){{Cite web|和書|title=銅製鰐口［どうせいわにぐち］元亀2nd year 銘等 - 岐阜県公式ホームページ（文化伝承課） |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12484.html#:~:text=%E6%85%B6%E9%95%B710%E5%B9%B4%20(1605)%E4%BD%9C%E3%81%AE%E9%87%8E%E7%80%AC%E9%B0%90%E5%8F%A3%E3%81%AF%E3%80%81%E9%8B%B3%E9%89%84%E8%A3%BD%E3%81%A7%E9%9D%A2%E5%BE%8427.5cm%E3%80%81%E5%8E%9A%E3%81%958.0cm%E3%81%A7%E3%81%82%E3%82%8B%E3%80%82,%E5%85%83%E4%BA%802%E5%B9%B4%20(1571)%E4%BD%9C%E3%81%AE%E9%B0%90%E5%8F%A3%E3%81%AF%E4%BF%A1%E9%95%B7%E9%B0%90%E5%8F%A3%E3%81%A8%E7%A7%B0%E3%81%95%E3%82%8C%E3%81%A6%E3%81%84%E3%82%8B%E3%80%82%20%E9%9D%A2%E5%BE%8436.0cm%E3%80%81%E5%8E%9A%E3%81%9510.0cm%E3%81%A7%E3%81%82%E3%82%8B%E3%80%82 |website=www.pref.gifu.lg.jp |access-date=2023-06-23}} * Bronze {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|qid=Q11674315|lt=Wani-kuchi|1=Waniguchi}} (donated by {{ill|Shibata Katsuie|en|Shibata Katsuie|qid=Q707587|lt=Shibata Katsuie|1=Shibata Katsuie}}) * Bronze Standing Statue of {{ill|Kongō Dōji|ja|金銅金剛童子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kongō Dōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}{{Cite web|和書|title=金銅金剛童子立像 - 岐阜県公式ホームページ（文化伝承課） |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/5572.html |website=www.pref.gifu.lg.jp |access-date=2023-08-04}} * Wooden Seated Statue of {{ill|Bhaisajyaguru|en|Bhaisajyaguru|qid=Q854773|lt=Yakushi Nyorai Medicine Buddha|1=Bhaisajyaguru}} (former principal deity of the Shinto shrine){{Cite web|和書|title=木造薬師如来坐像［もくぞうやくしにょらいざぞう］（大師講） - 岐阜県公式ホームページ（文化伝承課） |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12564.html |website=www.pref.gifu.lg.jp |access-date=2023-08-04}} === Gifu Prefecture Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Architecture) === * Main House of the Itoshiro Family Residence{{Cite web|和書|title=登録有形文化財（建造物） - 岐阜県公式ホームページ（文化伝承課） |url=https://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/page/12417.html |website=www.pref.gifu.lg.jp |access-date=2023-08-04}}{{Cite web|和書|title=石徹白家住宅主屋 文化遺産オンライン |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/191380 |website=bunka.nii.ac.jp |access-date=2023-08-04 |language=ja}} === Gifu Prefecture Designated Natural Monuments === The "Jōan Sugi" and the beech primeval forest on the shrine grounds were designated as Natural Monuments of Gifu Prefecture on November 13, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 49 (1974 AD).{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/gujyousi/itoshiro.html |title=石徹白の浄安スギ |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2011-04-18}}{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/tenki/gujyousi/buna.html |title=白山中居神社のブナ原生林 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2011-04-18}} * Jōan Sugi of Itoshiro * Beech Primeval Forest of Hakusan Chukyō Shrine === Gujo City Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Five-tier Kagura of Hakusan Chukyō Shrine{{Cite web|和書 |url=http://www.nponia.com/page21-gifu.htm |title=岐阜県の無形民俗文化財リスト |publisher=NPO法人無形民俗文化財アーカイブス |accessdate=2013-04-24}} == Transportation Access == ; Public Transportation * Ride the "Itoshiro Line" of {{ill|Hakuchō Transportation|ja|白鳥交通 (岐阜県)|lt=Hakuchō Transportation|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakuchō Transportation|1=Shirotori Transportation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q107100941}} from either {{ill|Mino Hakuchō Station|ja|美濃白鳥駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Hakuchō Station|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Mino-Shirotori Station|qid=Q6868885}} or {{ill|Hokunō Station|en|Hokunō Station|qid=Q5878299|lt=Hokuno Station|1=Hokunō Station}} on the {{ill|Etsumi-Nan Line|en|Etsumi-Nan Line|qid=Q4426321|lt=Echiminami Line|1=Etsumi-Nan Line}}; the stop "Uezaisho" at the line’s terminus is approximately 300 m to the north. Note that on Sundays and holidays the service is suspended, with only three round trips per day, and the midday service operates on a demand basis. ; By Car * From {{ill|Japan National Route 156|en|Japan National Route 156|qid=Q6156829|lt=National Route 156|1=Japan National Route 156}} and the {{ill|Michi-no-Eki Hakusan Bunka no Sato Nagataki|ja|道の駅白山文化の里長滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Michi-no-Eki Hakusan Bunka no Sato Nagataki|1=Michinoeki Hakusanbunka no Sato Nagataki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11641420}}, take {{ill|Gifu Prefecture Route 314 Itoshiro Maedani Line|ja|岐阜県道314号石徹白前谷線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu Prefecture Route 314 Itoshiro Maedani Line|1=Gifu Prefectural Road Route 314|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471689}} and then {{ill|Gifu/Fukui Prefecture Route 127 Hakusan Chukyō Shrine Asahi Line|ja|岐阜県道・福井県道127号白山中居神社朝日線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu/Fukui Prefecture Route 127 Hakusan Chukyō Shrine Asahi Line|1=Gifu Prefectural Road and Fukui Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471855}} for about 12 {{ill|Kilometre|en|Kilometre|qid=Q828224|lt=km|1=Kilometre}}, roughly a 20-minute drive. * It is located 8.8 km northwest of {{ill|Hokunō Station|en|Hokunō Station|qid=Q5878299|lt=Hokuno Station|1=Hokunō Station}} on the {{ill|Etsumi-Nan Line|en|Etsumi-Nan Line|qid=Q4426321|lt=Nagaragawa Railway Echiminami Line|1=Etsumi-Nan Line}}. * It is situated 11.6 km north-northwest of the {{ill|Takasu Interchange|ja|高鷲インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takasu Interchange|1=Takasu interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11673303}} on the {{ill|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|en|Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway|qid=Q7862674|lt=Tokai Hokuriku Expressway|1=Tōkai-Hokuriku Expressway}}. == Mino Zenjōdō == The Mino Zenjōdō, established by {{ill|Taichō|en|Taichō|qid=Q2388439|lt=Taichō|1=Taichō}}, is a route based on the tradition of Hakusan Faith leading from the Gifu Prefecture side to the zenith of ascetic training (the summit of {{ill|Mount Haku|en|Mount Haku|qid=Q2639098|lt=Hakusan|1=Mount Haku}}). Today, it is maintained as a mountain trail within {{ill|Hakusan National Park|en|Hakusan National Park|qid=Q1570081|lt=Hakusan National Park|1=Hakusan National Park}}, with numerous historic remains along the way – including the {{ill|National Special Natural Monument|ja|特別天然記念物|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/National Special Natural Monument|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天然記念物|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Natural monument|qid=Q23790}} {{ill|Great Sugi of Itoshiro|ja|石徹白の大杉|lt=Great Sugi of Itoshiro|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Sugi of Itoshiro|1=Itoshiro Cedar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11586523}}, training grounds, ruins of lodging facilities associated with ascetic practices, and toponyms steeped in the legends of {{ill|Taichō Daisō|ja|泰澄大師|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taichō Daisō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 泰澄|1=Taichō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2388439}} and others.{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.ishikawa.lg.jp/hakusan/publish/sizen/documents/sizen21.pdf |title=白山の禅定道 |format=PDF |publisher={{ill|Ishikawa Prefecture|en|Ishikawa Prefecture|qid=Q131281|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}} |pages=16-21 |accessdate=2011-10-22}}{{Cite web |url=https://www.bunka.go.jp/seisaku/bunkazai/shokai/kinenbutsu/rekishinomichi/pdf/92360601_01.pdf |title=３５．白山禅定道 ―加賀禅定道 ―越前禅定道 ―美濃禅定道 |access-date=2024-10-26 |publisher={{ill|Agency for Cultural Affairs|en|Agency for Cultural Affairs|qid=Q1009519|lt=Agency for Cultural Affairs|1=Agency for Cultural Affairs}}}} * The route: {{ill|Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja|長滱白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagataki Hakusan Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505741|1=Nagataki Hakusan Shrine}} – {{ill|Amida Falls|ja|阿弥陀ヶ滝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amida Falls|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿弥陀ケ滝|1=Amidaga Falls|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11657413}} – '''Hakusan Chukyō Shrine''' – {{ill|Great Sugi of Itoshiro|ja|石徹白の大スギ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Sugi of Itoshiro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135504614|1=Great Sugi of Itoshiro}} – Otakeri Slope – Ruins of Kamihato Shrine – Mother Stone – {{ill|Chōshi-ga-mine|ja|銚子ヶ峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōshi-ga-mine|1=Mount Chōshigamine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11650213}} – Ichinose – Ninohane – Mizu-tomi Gongen Shrine – {{ill|Sannomine|ja|三ノ峰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sannomine|1=sannomine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11354053}} – Mitaraike (Betsuyama area) – {{ill|Mount Bessan|en|Mount Bessan|qid=Q6919709|lt=Betsuyama Shrine|1=Mount Bessan}} – Iwamuro – Mitarai-yama – Heavenly Pond – Aburazaka – Nanryū Horse Ground – Tonbi Rock – {{ill|Murodō|en|Murodō|qid=Q11452989|lt=Shitsudō|1=Murodō}} – {{ill|Mount Haku|en|Mount Haku|qid=Q2639098|lt=Hakusan (Gozen-mine)|1=Mount Haku}} == Footnotes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == {{commonscat|Hakusanchukyo-jinja}} * {{ill|Hakusan Shrine|ja|白山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakusan Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Gifu/Fukui Prefecture Route 127 Hakusan Chukyō Shrine Asahi Line|ja|岐阜県道127号白山中居神社朝日線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gifu/Fukui Prefecture Route 127 Hakusan Chukyō Shrine Asahi Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 岐阜県道・福井県道127号白山中居神社朝日線|1=Gifu Prefectural Road and Fukui Prefectural Road Route 127|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11471855}} == External Links == * [https://kukuruwomeguru.com/hakusan-chykyo-shrine/ [Gujo City, Gifu Prefecture] Hakusan Chukyō Shrine (はくさんちゅうきょじんじゃ)] * [https://www.kankou-gifu.jp/spot/detail_3490.html Hakusan Chukyō Shrine] – Gifu Travel Guide {{shinto-stub}} {{Iwanaga Faith}} {{translated
